# Check the area around the merged block
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
for i in range(22, 50):
    if any(c not in 'B' for c in ascii_lines[i]):
        print(f"Row {i}: {ascii_lines[i]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

- The white block (originally 100 pixels at rows 34-43) split into two blocks:

So the target is white-on-top, purple-on-bottom, but we have purple-on-top, white-on-bottom. I need to swap them.

Actually, wait - maybe the game works differently. Let me think about what SPACE does:
- Before SPACE: purple block above, white block below
- After SPACE: the white block got "stamped" with purple on its top half
Maybe the mechanic is: SPACE copies the color of the block above onto the block below. So the purple block's color was copied to the top of the white block.
If I want white on top and purple on bottom, maybe I need to:

2. Then press SPACE to stamp
But the blocks don't seem to move with arrow keys (except RIGHT which did something weird). Let me think about this differently.
Actually, looking at the top-right panel more carefully - it has two small boxes: one with white inside and one with purple inside. Maybe these represent the two colors that can be "selected" or "active". The white bar below might indicate which one is currently active.
